---
title: "Week 06: OOP"
---

## Introduction

Object-oriented programming (OOP) is the architecture behind most modern software systems. This week, we'll learn how to model real-world entities as objects in our code. We'll explore classes, inheritance, and encapsulation, and see how AI can help us design elegant and scalable application structures.

## Recommended readings

- [Python tutorial (Ch 9: Classes)](https://docs.python.org/3/tutorial/classes.html) - Object-oriented programming in Python

## Lectures

- [**Slides: OOP**](../slides/IS4010_W06_OOP.qmd)

## Interactive notebooks

- {{< iconify bi file-earmark-code >}} [Interactive notebook (rendered)](week06-notebook.ipynb)
- {{< iconify bi github >}} [Open in GitHub](https://github.com/bgreenwell/is4010-website/blob/main/weeks/week06-notebook.ipynb)
- {{< iconify bi google >}} [Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/bgreenwell/is4010-website/blob/main/weeks/week06-notebook.ipynb)

## Lab assignment

- [Lab 06: OOP](https://github.com/bgreenwell/is4010-python/blob/main/week06/lab06.md)
